title: SP Deep Diagnostics — Automated Root Cause Analysis
description: Triggered by Davis Problems on database services. Gathers evidence from all monitoring layers and uses Davis CoPilot to produce a root cause narrative with actionable recommendations.

extract_problem_context.name: Extract Problem Context
extract_problem_context.description: Extract the affected entity, SP name, and define the analysis time window.

gather_latch_metrics.name: Gather Latch Metrics (Layer 1)
gather_latch_metrics.description: Query latch class metrics from the analysis time window.

input.query: timeseries latch_wait = avg(custom.mssql.latch.wait_time_ms), by: { latch_class }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | sort arrayAvg(latch_wait) desc | limit 20

gather_wait_metrics.name: Gather Wait Type Metrics (Layer 1)
gather_wait_metrics.description: Query wait type breakdown from the analysis time window.

input.query: timeseries wait = avg(custom.mssql.wait.wait_time_ms), by: { wait_type }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | sort arrayAvg(wait) desc | limit 20

timeseries { deficit = avg(custom.mssql.memory.grant_deficit_kb), waiting = avg(custom.mssql.memory.grant_waiting_count), wait_time = avg(custom.mssql.memory.grant_wait_time_ms) }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}"

gather_blocking_chains.name: Gather Blocking Chain Data (Layer 1)
gather_blocking_chains.description: Query active blocking chains with query text.

input.query: timeseries wait = max(custom.mssql.blocking.wait_time_ms), by: { blocked_spid, blocker_spid, wait_type, database_name, blocked_query, blocker_query }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | filter isNotNull(blocked_spid) | sort arrayMax(wait) desc

gather_autogrowth.name: Gather Auto-Growth Events (Layer 1)
gather_autogrowth.description: Query auto-growth events to correlate with latch spikes.

input.query: timeseries growth = sum(custom.mssql.autogrowth.event_count), by: { event_name, database_name, file_name }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | filter arraySum(growth) > 0

gather_proc_waits.name: Gather Proc Statement Waits (Layer 1)
gather_proc_waits.description: Query per-statement wait breakdown inside stored procedures.

input.query: timeseries total_wait = max(custom.mssql.proc_wait.total_wait_time_ms), by: { proc_name, query_sql_text, wait_category_desc }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | sort arrayMax(total_wait) desc | limit 20

gather_plan_regression.name: Gather Plan Regression Data (Layer 1)
gather_plan_regression.description: Query plan regression detection — multi-plan queries with high variance.

input.query: timeseries variance = max(custom.mssql.plan.variance_ratio), by: { proc_name, query_sql_text, query_id }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | sort arrayMax(variance) desc | limit 10

gather_io_latency.name: Gather File I/O Latency (Layer 1)
gather_io_latency.description: Query per-file I/O latency to identify storage bottlenecks.

input.query: timeseries { read_lat = avg(custom.mssql.io.read_latency_ms), write_lat = avg(custom.mssql.io.write_latency_ms) }, by: { database_name, file_name, file_type }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | sort arrayAvg(read_lat) desc

timeseries latch_wait = max(custom.mssql.index.page_latch_wait_ms), by: { database_name, table_name, index_name }, from: "{{result('extract_problem_context').from}}", to: "{{result('extract_problem_context').to}}" | sort arrayMax(latch_wait) desc | limit 10

gather_health_check.name: Gather Health Check Data (Layer 4)
gather_health_check.description: Query most recent missing indexes, stale statistics, and server config.

input.query: timeseries { missing_idx = max(custom.mssql.missing_index.improvement_score), frag = max(custom.mssql.fragmentation.avg_percent), stale = max(custom.mssql.stats.modification_percent) }, by: { table_name }, from: now() - 2h | sort arrayMax(missing_idx) desc | limit 15

gather_config_check.name: Gather Server Config (Layer 4)
gather_config_check.description: Query server configuration values for misconfiguration detection.

input.query: timeseries config_val = avg(custom.mssql.config.value), by: { config_name }, from: now() - 2h

copilot_rca.name: Davis CoPilot Root Cause Analysis
copilot_rca.description: Feed all gathered evidence to Davis CoPilot for automated root cause determination.

input.systemPrompt:
You are a Microsoft SQL Server performance expert working as a Dynatrace Davis CoPilot analyst. You have been given diagnostic evidence from multiple monitoring layers, all from the same time window surrounding a stored procedure performance degradation detected by Davis AI.

Your task is to:
1. Analyze the evidence and determine the root cause category
2. Build a causal chain connecting the evidence
3. Identify the specific trigger event
4. Identify the specific statement/query most impacted
5. Provide actionable recommendations to prevent recurrence

Use the following decision tree:

CHECK 1 - PARAMETER SNIFFING / PLAN REGRESSION
IF plan_regression data shows plan_variance_ratio > 5 AND multiple plan_ids exist
→ Root cause: Parameter sniffing or plan regression
→ Recommendations: OPTION(RECOMPILE), OPTIMIZE FOR UNKNOWN, plan guides

CHECK 2 - LATCH CONTENTION
IF latch metrics show spike AND (auto-growth event OR FGCB_ADD_REMOVE spike OR BUFFER latch spike)
→ Root cause: Latch contention caused by [specific trigger]
→ Recommendations: Pre-size files, add TempDB files, partition hot tables

CHECK 3 - LOCK BLOCKING
IF blocking chain data shows active blocking AND proc_wait shows wait_category = 'Lock'
→ Root cause: Lock blocking
→ Recommendations: Index optimization, query tuning, isolation level review

CHECK 4 - MEMORY PRESSURE / TEMPDB SPILLS
IF memory_grants shows wait_time > 0 OR ideal >> granted AND spill warnings detected
→ Root cause: Insufficient memory grant causing TempDB spills
→ Recommendations: UPDATE STATISTICS, OPTION(MIN_GRANT_PERCENT), increase max server memory

CHECK 5 - I/O BOTTLENECK
IF file I/O latency shows avg_read > 20ms or avg_write > 5ms AND PAGEIOLATCH spikes
→ Root cause: I/O bottleneck
→ Recommendations: Rebuild indexes, review storage subsystem

CHECK 6 - STALE STATISTICS
IF stale_statistics shows modification_percent > 20% AND missing_column_statistics XEvents
→ Root cause: Stale statistics causing bad cardinality estimates
→ Recommendations: UPDATE STATISTICS with FULLSCAN

CHECK 7 - CONFIGURATION ISSUES
IF MAXDOP=0 with CXPACKET waits, or tempdb_file_count=1 with FGCB latches
→ Root cause: Server misconfiguration
→ Recommendations: Specific config changes with values

CHECK 8 - RECOMPILATION OVERHEAD
IF recompile events are frequent AND CPU is dominant wait
→ Root cause: Excessive recompilation
→ Recommendations: WITH RECOMPILE on specific statements, review temp table usage

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Multiple root causes can coexist. List in order of severity.
- Always reference specific session IDs, query text, timestamps, and metric values.
- If evidence is insufficient, say so explicitly.
- Never guess without evidence.
- Format recommendations as specific, actionable steps with SQL examples.
- Start with a one-line summary, then provide detailed analysis.

post_rca_comment.name: Post RCA to Problem Card
post_rca_comment.description: Post the CoPilot root cause analysis as a comment on the Davis Problem.

body.message:
## SP Deep Diagnostics — Automated Root Cause Analysis

{{result('copilot_rca').response}}
